{"description":"Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math. Tests retry-after-failure workflow from gist §1.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running, post-checkout hook installed

## Test 1: Create branch and do work
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout -b attempt-1 (post-checkout hook registers with merge-with-branch)
3. bd create --title="First approach" --type=task --priority=1
4. echo "11 + 0 = 11" > sums/11.txt
5. echo "12 + 0 = 12" > sums/12.txt
6. git add -A && git commit -m "attempt 1 work"
7. bd update <id> --status=in_progress --notes="Working on first approach"

## Test 2: Simulate failure and reset
1. Record current state: git log --oneline -3
2. git checkout main
3. Observe: post-checkout hook fires — Dolt switches back to main
4. git branch -D attempt-1

## Test 3: Verify clean state
1. bd list — verify attempt-1 issues NOT visible on main (Strategy B isolation)
2. ls sums/ — verify sums/11.txt and sums/12.txt gone (git reset)

## Test 4: Retry with new approach
1. git checkout -b attempt-2 (post-checkout hook registers with merge-with-branch)
2. bd create --title="Second approach (better)" --type=task --priority=1
3. echo "11 + 1 = 12" > sums/11.txt
4. git add -A && git commit -m "attempt 2 work"
5. bd vc merge --from=attempt-2 --to=main --cleanup
6. git checkout main && bd list
7. Verify: only attempt-2 issue visible, no leftover from attempt-1

## Cleanup
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Strategy B branch can be abandoned cleanly
- [ ] No orphaned Dolt state from deleted branches
- [ ] New branch starts clean with no contamination from previous attempt
- [ ] Merge brings only the successful attempt to main"}

1. git checkout test/strategy-c
2. bd create --title="Issue to close" --type=task && bd create --title="Issue to leave open" --type=task
3. bd close <first-id> --reason="done"
4. git checkout main && bd list
5. Verify: closed issue visible on main, open issue still NOT visible

## Test 4: Reopen after merge
1. git checkout test/strategy-c
2. bd update <closed-id> --status=open
3. git checkout main && bd list
4. Observe: what state does main show for the reopened issue?

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D test/strategy-c
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] WIP issues invisible on main during development
- [ ] bd close triggers auto-merge to main
- [ ] Only closed issues propagate; open issues stay isolated
- [ ] Reopen behavior documented (edge case)"}

{"description":"Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server

## Test 1: Non-conflicting concurrent updates (cell-level merge)
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-on-close
2. git checkout -b test/conflict-a (post-checkout hook registers)
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b test/conflict-b (post-checkout hook registers)
4. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Conflict test issue" --type=task --priority=1
5. bd close <id> --reason="merge to main" (triggers auto-merge)
6. From test/conflict-b: bd update <id> --notes="Adding notes from branch B"
7. bd close <id-of-another-issue> --reason="merge to main"
8. Verify: both updates on main (cell-level merge, no conflict — different fields)

## Test 2: Conflicting updates (same cell)
1. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Same cell conflict" --type=task --priority=0
2. bd close <id> --reason="merge" (triggers auto-merge, gets to main)
3. From test/conflict-b: bd update <same-id> --priority=3
4. bd close <another-id> --reason="trigger merge"
5. Observe: merge conflict on priority field — should see warning in stderr
6. Verify: branch B's close still succeeds (work continues), merge skipped with warning

## Test 3: Self-healing retry
1. From test/conflict-b: make another bd update (any field)
2. bd close <yet-another-id> --reason="retry merge"
3. Observe: retry attempt — does it merge cleanly now?

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D test/conflict-a test/conflict-b
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Non-conflicting concurrent updates merge cleanly (cell-level)
- [ ] True conflicts (same cell) abort gracefully with warning
- [ ] Work continues on branch after failed merge
- [ ] Retry on next close attempts merge again
- [ ] Warning message clear enough for debugging"}

{"description":"Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server
- Cross-branch messaging implemented

## Test 1: Message delivery between Strategy B branches
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout -b test/msg-sender (post-checkout hook registers)
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b test/msg-receiver (post-checkout hook registers)
4. git checkout test/msg-sender
5. bd create --title="Need clarification on sums format" --type=message
6. git checkout test/msg-receiver && bd list --type=message
7. Verify: message arrived via cherry-pick

## Test 2: Message visible on main
1. git checkout main && bd list --type=message
2. Verify: message visible on main (cherry-picked to all active branches including main)

## Test 3: Reply from receiver
1. git checkout test/msg-receiver
2. bd create --title="Use format: X + Y = Z" --type=message
3. git checkout test/msg-sender && bd list --type=message
4. Verify: reply arrived on sender branch

## Test 4: Strategy A branch as receiver
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main
2. git checkout -b test/msg-stay (post-checkout hook registers with stay-on-main)
3. bd list --type=message
4. Verify: messages already visible (Strategy A reads from main)

## Test 5: Failed delivery does not break sender
1. Delete test/msg-receiver Dolt branch while messages in flight (if possible to time)
2. Send another message from test/msg-sender
3. Verify: sender unaffected, message exists on sender branch and main

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D test/msg-sender test/msg-receiver test/msg-stay
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Messages cherry-picked to all active branches
- [ ] Messages visible on main
- [ ] Replies propagate back to sender
- [ ] Strategy A branches see messages (already on main)
- [ ] Cherry-pick failure on one branch does not block others"}

{"description":"Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math (requires GitHub remote + branch protection).

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- GitHub remote configured with main branch protection
- Federation remote configured (DoltHub or local)

## Test 1: CI merge workflow
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout -b test/protected-pr (post-checkout hook registers)
3. bd create --title="Protected branch test" --type=task
4. echo "11 + 0 = 11" > differences/11.txt
5. git add -A && git commit -m "protected branch test"
6. git push origin test/protected-pr && bd dolt push
7. Open PR on GitHub
8. Merge PR via GitHub
9. Verify: beads-merge GitHub Action fires (if workflow installed)
10. bd dolt pull — verify main has the merged issues

## Test 2: PR rejection
1. Repeat steps 1-7 with new branch
2. Close PR without merging
3. Verify: Dolt main unaffected — no issue state leaked

## Cleanup
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] GitHub Action template works with bd vc merge --from/--to/--cleanup
- [ ] Merged PRs trigger Dolt merge
- [ ] Rejected PRs leave Dolt main clean
- [ ] .beads/refs/heads/main updated after CI merge"}

{"description":"Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math. Tests agent bake-off workflow from gist §1.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server, messaging enabled

## Test 1: Set up competing branches
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-1 (post-checkout hook registers)
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-2 (post-checkout hook registers)
4. git checkout main && git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-3 (post-checkout hook registers)

## Test 2: Each agent does independent work
1. git checkout bakeoff/agent-1
2. bd create --title="Agent 1 approach: sequential sums" --type=task
3. mkdir -p products && echo "sequential approach" > products/README.md
4. git add -A && git commit -m "agent 1 work"
5. Repeat for agent-2 and agent-3 with different approaches

## Test 3: Pick winner and merge
1. git checkout main
2. bd vc merge --from=bakeoff/agent-2 --to=main --cleanup
3. git merge bakeoff/agent-2 (git-side merge)
4. bd list — verify ONLY agent-2 issues visible on main

## Test 4: Discard losers
1. git branch -D bakeoff/agent-1 bakeoff/agent-3
2. bd list — verify NO ghost issues from agent-1 or agent-3
3. bd dolt sql "SELECT branch_name, status FROM beads_branches WHERE branch_name LIKE 'bakeoff/%'"
4. Verify: agent-2 status='merged', agent-1 and agent-3 need manual cleanup or remain 'active' (document which)

## Test 5: Messages preserved
1. Before discarding, send messages from bakeoff branches
2. After merge, verify messages from all branches preserved on main (cherry-picked during bake-off)

## Cleanup
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Winner's issues reach main via merge
- [ ] Loser branches leave no issue state on main
- [ ] Messages sent during bake-off preserved on main
- [ ] Clean workflow for discarding losing branches
- [ ] beads_branches registry reflects final state"}

{"description":"## Summary

Add `bd reset` command that wraps `git reset` and immediately runs mismatch detection, eliminating the lazy-detection gap where users must run a separate `bd` command after `git reset` to trigger Dolt sync.

Also add `bd check-refs` as a standalone explicit ref check command.

## Motivation

Phase 1 detection runs in PersistentPreRun (lazy). Users do `git reset --hard HEAD~1` then must remember to run `bd list` or similar to trigger the sync. This is confusing and error-prone. `bd reset` makes it one step.

## Design

### `bd reset` command
- Passes all arguments through to `git reset` (e.g., `bd reset --hard HEAD~1`)
- After git reset succeeds, immediately runs `checkBeadsRefSync()`
- Respects all `branch_strategy.*` settings (prompt, auto-reset, silent)
- Exits with git's exit code if git reset fails

### `bd check-refs` command
- Standalone explicit ref check (same as what PersistentPreRun does)

Add `bd reset` command that wraps `git reset` and immediately runs mismatch detection, eliminating the lazy-detection gap where users must run a separate `bd` command after `git reset` to trigger Dolt sync.

## Motivation

Phase 1 detection runs in PersistentPreRun (lazy). Users do `git reset --hard HEAD~1` then must remember to run `bd list` or similar to trigger the sync. This is confusing and error-prone. `bd reset` makes it one step.

## Design

### `bd reset` command
- Passes all arguments through to `git reset` (e.g., `bd reset --hard HEAD~1`)
- After git reset succeeds, immediately runs `checkBeadsRefSync()` 
- Respects all `branch_strategy.*` settings (prompt, auto-reset, silent)
- Exits with git's exit code if git reset fails

### `bd check-refs` command
- Standalone explicit ref check (same as what PersistentPreRun does)
- Useful for scripts, shell aliases, or manual verification
- Respects `branch_strategy.*` settings — does NOT force sync
- Exit code: 0 = in sync or action taken, 1 = mismatch detected but no action (silent mode)

### Git alias (optional, added by `bd init`)
- `bd init` offers to add `git breset` alias: `!bd reset`
- Users who prefer pure git get `git breset --hard HEAD~1` → same as `bd reset`
- Added to local .git/config, not global

### Lazy detection retained
- PersistentPreRun still runs `checkBeadsRefSync()` as fallback
- Catches cases where user does bare `git reset` without `bd reset`

## Shell integration options (documented, not implemented)
- Shell function wrapping `git` to intercept `git reset`
- Wrapper script in PATH
- These are user-configured, documented in README/gist","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T21:32:23Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:32:23Z"}

{"notes":"## Behavior Clarification (2026-03-07)

bd check-refs (and the check that runs after bd reset) follows this logic:

1. Compare .beads/refs hash against current Dolt commit hash
2. If match: do nothing (in sync)
3. If mismatch: check branch_strategy.* settings
   3a. If prompt disabled: follow the default strategy silently
       - reset_dolt_with_git=true → reset Dolt to match refs (keep histories in sync)
       - reset_dolt_with_git=false → allow git and Dolt histories to diverge
   3b. If prompt enabled: prompt user, with default strategy as the default answer
       - reset_dolt_with_git=true → default is Yes [Y/n] (reset)
       - reset_dolt_with_git=false → default is No [y/N] (allow divergence)
4. Execute the selected strategy: either reset Dolt or allow divergence

Exit codes: 0 always (all paths are intentional strategy execution). Non-zero only for errors (e.g. git reset fails).

## Remediation Items
- Fix check-refs help text (remove incorrect exit code 1 claim)
- Add tests for bd reset and bd check-refs"}

{"description":"Users who update bd (not fresh init) need two things migrated:

1. **Template comments**: config.yaml won't have branch_strategy section comments. bd doctor or bd init --update-config should detect and offer to add missing sections.

2. **Setting values**: If users previously set branch_strategy.* via bd config set (stored in Dolt config table), those values need to be migrated to config.yaml. Otherwise their preferences are silently lost on update.

Edge cases:
- What if config.yaml doesn't exist yet?
- What if they already have custom values in config.yaml?
- What if Dolt config and config.yaml disagree?
- What if they've never set any branch_strategy values (most users)?

Migration should be idempotent and non-destructive."}

{"description":"Existing users who upgrade bd won't have the branch_strategy section comments in their config.yaml (only written by bd init on fresh projects). This is a discoverability issue — the settings work fine without the comments (defaults are correct via config.GetBool returning false for missing keys), but users won't know the settings exist.

Options:
- bd doctor detects missing section and offers to append it
- bd init --update-config adds missing template sections to existing config.yaml
- Document in release notes / CHANGELOG only (lowest effort)

Should be idempotent and non-destructive — never overwrite existing user values."}

{"id":"bd-vlu.1.8","title":"Add branch_strategy.enabled setting to disable ref generation entirely","description":"Add an "off" option for beads refs via branch_strategy.enabled (default: true).

When branch_strategy.enabled=false:
- writeBeadsRefs() is a no-op (no .beads/HEAD or .beads/refs/ written)
- checkBeadsRefSync() is a no-op (no mismatch detection)
- bd reset still works but just wraps git reset without sync check
- bd check-refs reports "refs disabled" and exits 0

Use case: users who only want Strategy A (stay-on-main) forever and find ref file churn in git commits disruptive. This is effectively a permanent opt-out.

Decision on whether this must eventually be mandatory deferred to Phase 5 (UX polish): https://gist.github.com/bryanhirsch/5f003918e13a079975a27b5f7346fc37#145-phase-5--ux-polish-and-shell-integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T11:44:34Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-07T11:44:34Z"}

{"description":"## Summary

Beads refs are off by default. If no branch_strategy.* keys exist in config.yaml, no ref files are generated — equivalent to Strategy A without history tracking.

When any branch_strategy.* key is uncommented in config.yaml, the feature activates: ref files are written, mismatch detection runs, and bd reset/check-refs perform sync checks.

## Detection

IsBranchStrategyEnabled() in internal/config/config.go checks if any branch_strategy.* key is set via v.GetStringMap("branch_strategy"). Commented-out keys are invisible to viper.

## Guard points (4 places)

1. writeBeadsRefs() in dolt_head.go — early return if not enabled
2. checkBeadsRefSync() in dolt_head.go — early return if not enabled
3. bd reset Run in git_reset.go — skip sync check if not enabled (still wraps git reset)
4. bd check-refs Run in git_reset.go — print "refs disabled" to stderr if not enabled

## Cleanup on disable

When checkBeadsRefSync detects refs disabled but .beads/HEAD exists:
- git rm --cached the ref files (stage deletion)
- Print warning to stderr with suggested commit message
- Only trigger once (check file existence)

## Config template update

Update init_templates.go branch_strategy comments to explain:
1. Off by default, uncomment to enable
2. Which settings are active now vs forthcoming (checkout_dolt_with_git not yet supported)
3. Off = Strategy A without history tracking. On + Strategy A = history tracking enabling bd reset
4. Strategy A is the only supported strategy; B and C are forthcoming

## Tests

- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() returns false when no keys set, true when any key set
- writeBeadsRefs is a no-op when disabled
- checkBeadsRefSync is a no-op when disabled
- Cleanup stages deletion when ref files exist but feature disabled"}

{"id":"bd-vlu.1","title":"Phase 1: Beads Refs","description":"Pointer files (.beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/<branch>), mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun, branch_strategy.* config settings, and writeBeadsRefs integration into commit paths. Purely additive — records Dolt state into git-tracked files. All settings default to false — zero user-visible behavior changes.

## Revised Approach (2026-03-06)

Phase 1 pairs `git reset` with `DOLT_RESET` — both destructive operations that move the branch pointer. `git checkout` (non-destructive, detached HEAD) is deferred to Phase 2 (Strategy B) because Dolt has no native detached HEAD and supporting it requires temporary branch creation.

Mismatch detection runs in PersistentPreRun (every `bd` command), NOT in the post-checkout hook. This ensures:
1. Correct semantic pairing (reset ↔ reset, not checkout ↔ reset)
2. Reliable Dolt store context (no ensureStoreActive() workaround)
3. No false positives from git checkout

## Configuration

Four settings under branch_strategy.* namespace, all default false:
- branch_strategy.default_strategy (stay-on-main)
- branch_strategy.prompt (false — silent mode)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git (false — no auto-reset)
- branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git (false — Phase 2)

## Remaining Work

- **bd-vlu.1.7** (P1): Move detection from post-checkout hook to PersistentPreRun, implement branch_strategy.* settings
- **bd-vlu.1.3**: Re-run manual validation after the code change
- **bd-vlu.1.4**: Update automated tests for the new entry point and settings
- **bd-vlu.1.5**: Update CHANGELOG
- **bd-vlu.1.6**: Review contribution guidelines and submit PR

## Gate

Refs write correctly on every Dolt commit. All four branch_strategy settings combinations work correctly. git reset --hard followed by any bd command respects settings (silent by default, prompt or auto-reset when enabled). Round-trip integrity confirmed (reset backward then forward, no data loss or duplication).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:09:04Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-09T17:46:03Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T17:46:03Z","close_reason":"Phase 1 complete — all children closed, PR #2473 submitted to steveyegge/beads"}

{"description":"## Problem

In `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`, when `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK` fails with conflicts during cross-branch message delivery, the cleanup calls:

```go
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")
```

This is wrong. `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` only aborts in-progress merges, not cherry-picks. After a failed cherry-pick, no merge is in progress, so this call is a silent no-op — leaving the target branch's working set dirty with conflict markers.

The connection close (`defer deliveryDB.Close()`) provides an accidental safety net because Dolt discards uncommitted session state on disconnect, but relying on implicit cleanup is fragile.

## Root Cause

Copy-paste from `branch_strategy.go:70` which correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after a failed `DOLT_MERGE()`.

## Fix

Replace with `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`, which Dolt supports as the purpose-built cleanup:

```go
// Before (wrong):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")

// After (correct):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')")
```

Per Dolt docs: DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort') "Abort the current conflict resolution process, and revert all changes from the in-process cherry-pick operation."

## Test Plan

### Unit test: `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`

File: `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go` (new file)

Requires Dolt server (uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` — needs isolated DB since test creates branches and cherry-picks).

**Test scenario:**
1. Create a test store with isolated DB, create an issue on main, commit
2. Register a branch with `merge-with-branch` strategy (creates Dolt branch via `RegisterBranch`)
3. On the branch: modify the same issue to create a conflicting state, commit
4. On main: modify the same issue differently, commit — get the commit hash
5. Open a merge connection (`openMergeConnection`), call `cherryPickToTarget` to cherry-pick main's commit onto the branch
6. **Assert:** cherry-pick returns an error containing "conflict"
7. **Assert:** after the error, the branch working set is clean (verify via `SELECT * FROM dolt_status` returning no dirty tables)

This test verifies that `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')` actually cleans up, rather than leaving dirty state.

### Verification of correct usage in `branch_strategy.go`

No test needed — `mergeOnClose` at line 70 correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after `DOLT_MERGE()`. Just a manual review confirmation.

## Implementation Plan

1. **Fix the bug** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`: change `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` to `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`
2. **Write test** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go`:
   - `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`: Uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` + `openMergeConnection` to exercise the real cherry-pick conflict path against Dolt
   - Build tag: `//go:build cgo` (matches existing test files)
   - Skips if test Dolt server not available
3. **Verify** — Run `go test ./cmd/bd/ -run TestCherryPickToTarget -v` against test Dolt server

## References

- Dolt SQL Procedures docs: https://docs.dolthub.com/sql-reference/version-control/dolt-sql-procedures
- Introduced in commit 9bdc176c (this PR branch)
- Compare with correct usage in `branch_strategy.go:70`"}

{"description":"## Summary

The Phase 1 PR (feature/beads-refs) ships ~1500 lines of Phase 2+ code that must be removed before merge. Phase 2 code is preserved in feature/branch-strategies branch (see bd-vlu.2.16). This bead focuses on deletion and test hardening only.

Per roadmap §14.1: "No beads_branches table, no strategy selection, no merge machinery, no messaging, no git checkout time-travel."

## Worktree

Work branch: bd-vlu.1.18--refactor (worktree at bd-vlu.1.18--refactor/)

## What stays (Phase 1 scope)

- writeBeadsRefs / readBeadsRefs / cleanupStaleBeadsRefs (dolt_head.go)
- checkBeadsRefSync / mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun (dolt_head.go)
- bd reset command (git_reset.go)
- isTerminal() (hooks.go) — used by reset prompt
- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() (config.go) — presence-based check
- branch_strategy.prompt config (reset prompt only)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git config
- .gitattributes entries for .beads/refs

## Deletion plan

### Files to delete entirely

| File | Lines | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| cmd/bd/branch.go | ~172 | bd branch command, validStrategies, resolveStrategy() — Phase 2 |
| cmd/bd/branch_strategy.go | ~135 | executePostCommitStrategy(), mergeOnClose(), maybeMergeOnClose() — Phase 2 |
| cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go | ~122 | deliverMessageToActiveBranches(), maybeSendMessage() — Phase 2/4 |
| internal/storage/dolt/migrations/009_beads_branches.go | ~38 | beads_branches table — Phase 2 |

### Functions to delete from existing files

| File | Function/Section | Action |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| hooks.go | maybePromptBranchStrategy() (~70 lines) | Delete function entirely |
| store.go | BranchInfo struct, RegisterBranch(), GetBranchInfo(), ListRegisteredBranches(), DeleteBranch(), UnregisterBranch() | Delete all |

### Call sites to remove

| File | Line(s) | Call | Action |
|------|---------|------|--------|
| dolt_head.go | 147, 176 | maybePromptBranchStrategy(ctx, s) | Delete call |
| dolt_autocommit.go | 24, 78 | executePostCommitStrategy(ctx, s/st) | Delete call |
| close.go | 148 | maybeMergeOnClose(ctx, store) | Delete call |
| checkout.go | 32-39, 46-48 | GetBranchInfo() + strategy display | Delete |
| branch.go | 170 | rootCmd.AddCommand(branchCmd) | Deleted with file |
| migrations.go | ~30 | beads_branches migration registration | Delete entry |

### Config references to remove

- branch_strategy.default_strategy — all references (hooks.go:882, branch.go)
- branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git — if referenced anywhere

## Test plan

### Tests to delete
- Any tests for deleted functions (RegisterBranch, GetBranchInfo, maybePromptBranchStrategy, etc.)

### Tests to add/verify
1. Verify bd reset works independently with no branch registry
2. Verify mismatch detection works without strategy prompt
3. Verify close command works without merge-on-close call
4. Verify auto-commit works without post-commit strategy dispatch
5. Verify IsBranchStrategyEnabled() returns false with empty config
6. Verify writeBeadsRefs still works after cleanup
7. Build succeeds with no compilation errors from removed code

## Verification

After all deletions:
1. go build ./cmd/bd/ — must compile
2. go test ./cmd/bd/ — all remaining tests pass
3. go test ./internal/... — all remaining tests pass
4. git diff --stat against feature/beads-refs — confirm ~1500 lines removed"}

{"id":"bd-vlu.1.18","title":"Remove Phase 2+ code from Phase 1 PR: strategy prompt, branch registry, merge machinery","description":"## Bug

The Phase 1 PR (feature/beads-refs) ships ~1500 lines of Phase 2+ code that should not be in this release. Per the roadmap (https://gist.github.com/bryanhirsch/5f003918e13a079975a27b5f7346fc37, §14.1), Phase 1 scope is limited to:

- .beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/ pointer files
- writeBeadsRefs call sites
- bd reset command
- bd check-refs command (not yet implemented)
- Mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun
- branch_strategy.prompt config (reset prompt only, NOT strategy selection prompt)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git config
- Interactive reset prompt (§8.3 — when mismatch detected after git reset)

Phase 1 explicitly states: "No beads_branches table, no strategy selection, no merge machinery, no messaging, no git checkout time-travel."

## What must be removed

### 1. branch_strategy.enabled is cruft
branch_strategy is enabled if ANY branch_strategy.* key is found in config.yaml. The explicit enabled flag should not exist or be referenced anywhere. IsBranchStrategyEnabled() in config.go already checks len(v.GetStringMap("branch_strategy")) > 0 — that's correct. Remove any references to an explicit enabled key.

### 2. Strategy selection prompt (hooks.go:861-933)
maybePromptBranchStrategy() is Phase 2. The A/B/C strategy selection prompt should be removed entirely. The only prompt in Phase 1 is the reset prompt (§8.3) — "Dolt is behind git. Reset Dolt? [Y/n]". Remove this function and all call sites.

### 3. beads_branches table and branch registration
- Migration 009_beads_branches.go creates a Phase 2 table
- RegisterBranch(), GetBranchInfo(), ListRegisteredBranches() in store.go are Phase 2
- All callers of these APIs need to be removed or have the calls removed
- Decision needed: leave the migration in place (dormant table) or remove it? Removing is cleaner but means Phase 2 needs to re-add it. Leaving it is harmless but ships dead schema.

### 4. Merge machinery (branch_strategy.go)
- executePostCommitStrategy() — Phase 2 dispatch on merge strategies. Called from transact() and maybeAutoCommit() in dolt_autocommit.go. Remove calls.
- mergeOnClose() — Phase 2 merge logic
- maybeMergeOnClose() — called from close.go:148. Remove call.
- The entire branch_strategy.go file may be removable if only Phase 2 code remains after cleanup.

### 5. bd branch command (branch.go)
Entire file is Phase 2: branch listing with strategies, branch creation with --strategy flag, validStrategies map, resolveStrategy(), strategyShortcut(). Remove command registration.

### 6. Cross-branch messaging (branch_messaging.go)
Entire file is Phase 2/4: deliverMessageToActiveBranches(), maybeSendMessage(). Remove file and all call sites.

### 7. Checkout strategy display (checkout.go:32, 46-47)
GetBranchInfo() call and strategy display are Phase 2. Remove.

### 8. Config settings that are Phase 2
- branch_strategy.default_strategy — Phase 2
- branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git — Phase 2
- branch_strategy.prompt for strategy selection — Phase 2 (note: branch_strategy.prompt for the RESET prompt is Phase 1)

### 9. Post-checkout hook code
Any post-checkout hook logic related to branch strategy selection or registration is Phase 2. bd reset is Phase 1 and should work independently. If there's good Phase 2 code here, it can be saved in a different branch but should not ship in Phase 1.

## What must stay

- writeBeadsRefs / readBeadsRefs / cleanupStaleBeadsRefs (dolt_head.go)
- checkBeadsRefSync / mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun
- bd reset command (git_reset.go)
- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() config check (presence-based, not explicit flag)
- branch_strategy.prompt for the reset prompt (§8.3)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git config
- All tests for the above

## Approach

This is a narrowing/removal task. Good Phase 2 code can be preserved in the feature/branch-strategies branch when Phase 2 begins. The goal is to make the Phase 1 PR match the roadmap scope exactly.

## Config template (Phase 1 only, per roadmap §14.1)

```yaml
# branch_strategy:
#   prompt: true
#   defaults:
#     reset_dolt_with_git: false
```

No default_strategy, no checkout_dolt_with_git, no enabled flag.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-10T13:50:36Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-10T13:50:36Z"}

Work branch: bd-vlu.1.18--refactor (worktree at bd-vlu.1.18--refactor/)

## Key requirements (from review)

1. **branch_strategy.enabled is cruft** — it should not exist or be referenced anywhere. branch_strategy is enabled if ANY branch_strategy.* key is found in config.yaml. IsBranchStrategyEnabled() already does this correctly (presence-based). Verify no explicit "enabled" key exists in code, config templates, or docs.

2. **No managed branching strategies until Phase 2** — beads should not disallow ad hoc branching, but bd reset must work independently of any branching strategy. Remove all strategy selection, registration, and merge machinery.

3. **Strategy prompt must be removed** — the A/B/C strategy selection prompt (maybePromptBranchStrategy) is Phase 2. The only prompt in Phase 1 is the reset prompt (§8.3): "Dolt is behind git. Reset Dolt? [Y/n]". Remove and re-implement in Phase 2.

4. **No git checkout hooks unless related to bd reset** — the post-checkout hook (runPostCheckoutHook) is already a stub (runs chained hooks, returns 0) and doesn't call Phase 2 code. It stays. The Phase 2 calls (maybePromptBranchStrategy) are in dolt_head.go's checkBeadsRefSync, not the hook — those calls get deleted.

## What stays (Phase 1 scope)

- writeBeadsRefs / readBeadsRefs / cleanupStaleBeadsRefs (dolt_head.go)
- checkBeadsRefSync / mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun (dolt_head.go)
- bd reset command (git_reset.go)
- isTerminal() (hooks.go) — used by reset prompt
- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() (config.go) — presence-based check
- runPostCheckoutHook() (hooks.go) — stub, runs chained hooks only
- branch_strategy.prompt config (reset prompt only)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git config
- .gitattributes entries for .beads/refs

| File | Lines | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| cmd/bd/branch.go | ~172 | bd branch command, validStrategies, resolveStrategy() — Phase 2 |
| cmd/bd/branch_strategy.go | ~135 | executePostCommitStrategy(), mergeOnClose(), maybeMergeOnClose() — Phase 2 |
| cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go | ~122 | deliverMessageToActiveBranches(), maybeSendMessage() — Phase 2/4 |
| internal/storage/dolt/migrations/009_beads_branches.go | ~38 | beads_branches table — Phase 2 |

### Functions to delete from existing files

| File | Function/Section | Action |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| hooks.go | maybePromptBranchStrategy() (~70 lines) | Delete function entirely |
| store.go | BranchInfo struct, RegisterBranch(), GetBranchInfo(), ListRegisteredBranches(), DeleteBranch(), UnregisterBranch() | Delete all |

| File | Line(s) | Call | Action |
|------|---------|------|--------|
| dolt_head.go | 147, 176 | maybePromptBranchStrategy(ctx, s) | Delete call |
| dolt_autocommit.go | 24, 78 | executePostCommitStrategy(ctx, s/st) | Delete call |
| close.go | 148 | maybeMergeOnClose(ctx, store) | Delete call |
| checkout.go | 32-39, 46-48 | GetBranchInfo() + strategy display | Delete |
| branch.go | 170 | rootCmd.AddCommand(branchCmd) | Deleted with file |
| migrations.go | ~30 | beads_branches migration registration | Delete entry |

### Config references to remove

- branch_strategy.default_strategy — all references (hooks.go:882, branch.go)
- branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git — verify none exist
- branch_strategy.enabled — verify no explicit key exists anywhere (code, templates, docs)

## Test plan

### Tests to delete
- Any tests for deleted functions (RegisterBranch, GetBranchInfo, maybePromptBranchStrategy, etc.)

### Tests to add/verify
1. Verify bd reset works independently with no branch registry
2. Verify mismatch detection works without strategy prompt
3. Verify close command works without merge-on-close call
4. Verify auto-commit works without post-commit strategy dispatch
5. Verify IsBranchStrategyEnabled() returns false with empty config
6. Verify writeBeadsRefs still works after cleanup
7. Build succeeds with no compilation errors from removed code

## Verification

After all deletions:

3. go test ./internal/... -count=1 — all remaining tests pass


No default_strategy, no checkout_dolt_with_git, no enabled flag."}

{"id":"bd-vlu.1.17","title":"Bug: cherry-pick conflict cleanup uses DOLT_MERGE('--abort') instead of DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')","description":"## Problem

In `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`, when `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK` fails with conflicts during cross-branch message delivery, the cleanup calls:

```go
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")
```

This is wrong. `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` only aborts in-progress merges, not cherry-picks. After a failed cherry-pick, no merge is in progress, so this call is a silent no-op — leaving the target branch's working set dirty with conflict markers.

The connection close (`defer deliveryDB.Close()`) provides an accidental safety net because Dolt discards uncommitted session state on disconnect, but relying on implicit cleanup is fragile.

## Root Cause

Copy-paste from `branch_strategy.go:70` which correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after a failed `DOLT_MERGE()`.

## Fix

Replace with `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`, which Dolt supports as the purpose-built cleanup:

```go
// Before (wrong):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")

// After (correct):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')")
```

Per Dolt docs: DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort') "Abort the current conflict resolution process, and revert all changes from the in-process cherry-pick operation."

## Test Plan

### Unit test: `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`

File: `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go` (new file)

Requires Dolt server (uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` — needs isolated DB since test creates branches and cherry-picks).

**Test scenario:**
1. Create a test store with isolated DB, create an issue on main, commit
2. Register a branch with `merge-with-branch` strategy (creates Dolt branch via `RegisterBranch`)
3. On the branch: modify the same issue to create a conflicting state, commit
4. On main: modify the same issue differently, commit — get the commit hash
5. Open a merge connection (`openMergeConnection`), call `cherryPickToTarget` to cherry-pick main's commit onto the branch
6. **Assert:** cherry-pick returns an error containing "conflict"
7. **Assert:** after the error, the branch working set is clean (verify via `SELECT * FROM dolt_status` returning no dirty tables)

This test verifies that `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')` actually cleans up, rather than leaving dirty state.

### Verification of correct usage in `branch_strategy.go`

No test needed — `mergeOnClose` at line 70 correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after `DOLT_MERGE()`. Just a manual review confirmation.

## Implementation Plan

1. **Fix the bug** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`: change `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` to `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`
2. **Write test** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go`:
   - `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`: Uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` + `openMergeConnection` to exercise the real cherry-pick conflict path against Dolt
   - Build tag: `//go:build cgo` (matches existing test files)
   - Skips if test Dolt server not available
3. **Verify** — Run `go test ./cmd/bd/ -run TestCherryPickToTarget -v` against test Dolt server

## References

- Dolt SQL Procedures docs: https://docs.dolthub.com/sql-reference/version-control/dolt-sql-procedures
- Introduced in commit 9bdc176c (this PR branch)
- Compare with correct usage in `branch_strategy.go:70`","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-10T13:35:08Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-10T14:05:36Z"}

{"notes":"## /simplify Review Results (PR #1)

Ran 3 parallel review agents (code reuse, code quality, efficiency) on the full PR diff (4269 additions, 370 deletions, 120+ files).

### Actionable Findings

**1. API key fallback pattern duplicated in 3 files (Code Reuse)**
- compact.go, find_duplicates.go, haiku.go all implement: env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY > config ai.api_key
- All 3 are PR changes (the PR added the config fallback)
- Fix: Extract config.GetAnthropicAPIKey() shared helper
- Severity: Medium — DRY violation, all in PR scope

**2. containsString() unnecessary wrapper (Code Reuse)**
- hooks_checkout_test.go:422-425 wraps slices.Contains() with a named function
- The codebase uses slices.Contains() directly elsewhere (e.g., main.go:373)
- Fix: Remove wrapper, use slices.Contains() directly at 3 call sites
- Severity: Low — test-only, cosmetic

**3. writeBeadsRefs() silently swallows errors (Code Quality)**
- dolt_head.go has 4 silent 'return // best effort' without any logging
- MkdirAll permissions failure would be invisible
- Fix: Add debug.Logf() before each silent return
- Severity: Medium — observability gap, debugging pain

**4. cleanupStaleBeadsRefs() uses 2 git rm subprocesses (Efficiency)**
- dolt_head.go:276-282 runs git rm twice (HEAD then refs/)
- Fix: Combine into single git rm call with both paths
- Severity: Low — rare code path (only during config transition)

### Confirmed Safe (No Action Needed)

- Slice append in git_reset.go — not an aliasing bug
- Shell injection in generateHookSection() — hookName from compile-time constants
- isTerminal() — correct TTY detection, intentionally different from ui.IsTerminal()
- checkBeadsRefSync on all commands — nil-guarded, safe no-op for non-DB commands
- writeBeadsRefs double-call — mitigated by lastWrittenRefs dedup guard
- vc merge auto-commit — protected by commandDidExplicitDoltCommit flag
- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() — GetStringMap correctly detects nested keys
- isRebaseInProgress() git call — mitigated by sync.Once caching in internal/git
- lastWrittenRefs thread safety — low risk, single-command-per-process invariant
- getDoltCommitMessage uses dolt_commits not dolt_log — intentional for forward-reset case"}

{"notes":"## /simplify Findings (PR #1)

### Scope
Only findings from code authored by Bryan Hirsch in this PR. Finding #1 (API key consolidation) was excluded — git blame confirmed it belongs to Joseph Bath (commit 33ce6bf0), not our PR.

### Implemented Fixes

**Fix #2: Remove containsString() wrapper** (hooks_checkout_test.go)
- Removed trivial wrapper that just called slices.Contains()
- Replaced 3 call sites (lines 218, 285, 288) with direct slices.Contains() calls

**Fix #3: Add debug logging to silent returns** (dolt_head.go:writeBeadsRefs)
- Added debug.Logf() before 4 silent 'return // best effort' statements
- Errors in best-effort code are now visible with BD_DEBUG=1

**Fix #4: Combine git rm subprocess calls** (dolt_head.go:cleanupStaleBeadsRefs)
- Merged 2 separate 'git rm --cached' calls into 1 (git rm accepts multiple paths)
- Eliminates an unnecessary subprocess spawn"}

{"description":"## Summary

Create an E2E integration test that exercises `bd reset --hard HEAD~1` against a real git repo with a real Dolt container, verifying both git and Dolt state move backward together, then forward for round-trip integrity.

## Context / Gap Analysis

**Existing coverage:**
- `TestAutoResetRoundTrip` (git_reset_test.go:386) — tests Dolt reset round-trip via `writeTestBeadsRefs` + `checkBeadsRefSync` against a real Docker Dolt container. Verifies issue creation, backward reset (issue disappears), forward reset (issue returns), and `getDoltCommitMessage` for forward-unreachable commits. **Does NOT exercise the CLI or git repo layer.**
- `TestCleanupStaleBeadsRefsInGitRepo` (git_reset_test.go:489) — tests ref file cleanup via `git rm --cached` inside a real git repo. **Does NOT exercise Dolt at all.**

**Gap:** No test exercises the full `bd reset` CLI command path (git reset → ref mismatch detection → Dolt reset) inside a real git repo with a real Dolt container.

## Technical Architecture

**Location:** `cmd/bd/git_reset_test.go` (alongside existing reset tests)

**Infrastructure used (all existing — no new infra needed):**
- `testutil.StartIsolatedDoltContainer(t)` — per-test Dolt container with auto-cleanup
- `newTestStoreWithPrefix(t, dbPath, prefix)` — creates DoltStore against test container
- `config.Initialize()` + `config.Set("branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git", true)` — enables ref sync
- `writeTestBeadsRefs(t, beadsDir, branch, hash)` — simulates what a git reset does to refs
- `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)` — triggers mismatch detection and Dolt reset

**Approach:** Internal function-level test (NOT subprocess). Uses the same pattern as `TestAutoResetRoundTrip` but adds a real git repo layer:
1. Create temp git repo (`git init` + `git config`)
2. Place `.beads/` inside the git repo
3. Create issues, commit to Dolt, commit to git
4. Simulate `bd reset --hard HEAD~1` by: writing beads refs with the prior Dolt hash, then calling `checkBeadsRefSync`
5. Verify git state (via git log) and Dolt state (via store queries)

**Why not subprocess:** The `bd reset` command calls `git reset` + `checkBeadsRefSyncWithGitLine`. Testing via subprocess requires building the `bd` binary, managing PATH, and dealing with process isolation. The function-level approach tests the same logic paths with better isolation and faster execution — consistent with `TestAutoResetRoundTrip`.

## Test Plan

### Test: `TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo`

**Setup:**
1. `t.TempDir()` → create temp dir
2. `git init` + `git config user.email/name` inside temp dir
3. Create `.beads/` dir, initialize DoltStore via `newTestStoreWithPrefix`
4. Enable branch_strategy via config
5. Initial git commit (empty or with a file)

**Phase 1 — Build two commits:**
1. Create issue A in Dolt, `store.Commit()`, record `checkpointHash`
2. Write beads refs, `git add .beads/`, `git commit -m "checkpoint"`
3. Create issue B in Dolt, `store.Commit()`, record `latestHash`
4. Write beads refs, `git add .beads/`, `git commit -m "latest"`

**Phase 2 — Backward reset:**
1. `git reset --hard HEAD~1` (moves git HEAD back)
2. Verify git log shows checkpoint commit at HEAD
3. Read `.beads/refs/heads/main` — should now have `checkpointHash` (restored by git reset)
4. Call `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)` — should detect mismatch and reset Dolt
5. Verify `store.GetCurrentCommit()` == `checkpointHash`
6. Verify `store.GetIssue(ctx, issueB.ID)` returns not-found
7. Verify `store.GetIssue(ctx, issueA.ID)` succeeds

**Phase 3 — Forward reset:**
1. Write beads refs with `latestHash`, `git add .beads/`, `git commit`, OR simulate by writing refs directly
2. Call `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)`
3. Verify `store.GetCurrentCommit()` == `latestHash`
4. Verify both issues A and B exist

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Test passes with Docker available (real Dolt container)
- [ ] Test skips gracefully when Docker unavailable (`skipIfNoDolt` pattern)
- [ ] Both git state and Dolt state verified at each step
- [ ] Round-trip integrity: backward then forward preserves all data
- [ ] No new test infrastructure or helpers needed

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Write the test function (~50-80 lines)

1. Add `TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo` to `cmd/bd/git_reset_test.go`
2. Setup: temp dir, git init, git config, create .beads dir
3. Create DoltStore via `newTestStoreWithPrefix` (skips if no Dolt)
4. Enable `branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git` via config
5. Create two Dolt commits with issues, writing beads refs and git committing at each step
6. Backward reset: `git reset --hard HEAD~1`, verify git log, call `checkBeadsRefSync`, verify Dolt state
7. Forward reset: write refs with latest hash, call `checkBeadsRefSync`, verify both issues exist
8. Cleanup via t.Cleanup (config reset)

### Phase 2: Verify

1. Run test with Docker: `go test -run TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo -v ./cmd/bd/ -count=1`
2. Run test without Docker: verify graceful skip message
3. Run full reset test suite: `go test -run TestReset -v ./cmd/bd/ -count=1`"}

This covers the gap identified in PR review.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:32:55Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:33:24Z"}

{"id":"bd-nrr","title":"Rebase feature/beads-refs: tidy 87 commits into 4 logical groups","description":"The feature/beads-refs branch has 87 commits including 14 merge commits from main,
~11 unrelated fixes already in main, backup noise, and a commit+revert pair. This
makes the PR (GH#2473) hard to review. Rebase to produce 4 clean squashed commits:

1. **feat: beads refs — track git-dolt history with branch_strategy config** (core impl, ~15 commits)
2. **feat: add bd reset and bd check-refs commands** (new commands, 4 commits)
3. **test: beads refs round-trip, E2E, and behavioral tests** (all tests, 7 commits)
4. **docs: CHANGELOG, config templates, and branch_strategy documentation** (docs, 5 commits)

## Plan

1. Rebase onto main (drops merge commits and already-merged fixes automatically)
2. Drop noise: 2 backup commits, 2 .beads/backup resets, 1 commit+revert pair
3. Interactive rebase to squash remaining ~35 commits into 4 groups

## Potential Complication

The rebase onto main may hit conflicts since many commits touch the same files
(hooks.go, init_templates.go, vc.go, config.go). If conflicts are extensive or
cascading, the interactive rebase becomes impractical.

## Backup Plan

- `feature/beads-refs-v2` branch preserves the original HEAD as a backup
- If rebase conflicts are unmanageable: abandon rebase, `git reset --hard feature/beads-refs-v2`
- Alternative approach: create a fresh branch from main, `git merge --squash feature/beads-refs-v2`
  to get all changes as a single diff, then selectively commit in 4 groups using `git add -p`
- Worst case: squash-merge the PR as-is (GitHub "Squash and merge" button)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bryan@bryanhirsch.com","created_at":"2026-03-10T22:00:18Z","created_by":"Bryan Hirsch","updated_at":"2026-03-10T22:00:18Z"}
